Q: Find the sum of the squares of the first 40 positive integers.
A: To find the sum of the squares of the first 40 positive integers, we can use the formula:

Σ(n^2) = n(n + 1)(2n + 1) / 6

For n = 40:

Σ(40^2) = 40(40 + 1)(2*40 + 1) / 6
Σ(1600) = 40 * 41 * 81 / 6
Σ(1600) = 16240

The sum of the squares of the first 40 positive integers is 16240.
